visual_features.overall_style: Clean, minimalist gallery aesthetic with strong black-and-white branding. The look is modern and editorial, relying on typography and whitespace rather than heavy decoration. Subtle color is only introduced via social media icons and artwork imagery, reinforcing a neutral, museum-like atmosphere.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is created primarily through type scale, weight, and alignment. The black wordmark logo is the strongest focal point, centered at the top. Below, the horizontal navigation in smaller, lighter text forms a clear secondary level. A thin horizontal rule then separates the global header from the page content. The centered page title '2025 Exhibitions' is the main content focal point, with bold, left-aligned exhibition titles (e.g., 'Uniqueness') and dates forming the next level. Image + text exhibition blocks use image weight on the left and bold headings on the right to attract attention sequentially: image → exhibition title → dates → body copy.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Whitespace is generous and intentional. Large vertical gaps separate logo, navigation, page title, and exhibition list. Side margins are wide, creating a centered content column with ample breathing room. Individual elements (logo, nav links, headings) are given clear space buffers, avoiding crowding and enhancing readability.
unique_elements: Bold black-and-white typographic logo with a block 'M' in a black rectangle juxtaposed against the rest of the wordmark.
Very lowercase, evenly spaced navigation labels contributing to a calm, understated header.
Fixed-position vertical social media buttons on the right edge (circular outlines with brand-colored borders) that float independently of the main content column.
Exhibition entries that pair a left-aligned artwork thumbnail with a right-hand text block in a simple two-column composition.

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Implicit centered column grid, likely a 12-column underlying system with the content constrained to a fixed-width or max-width container and generous left/right margins.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Centered single-column layout for global elements (logo, navigation, page title) followed by a two-column content pattern for exhibition entries (image on the left, text on the right).
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Top section: logo. Immediately below: primary navigation bar. Then a full-width horizontal rule. Next: a centered page heading ('2025 Exhibitions'). Another horizontal rule separates the heading from the exhibition list. Below this, exhibitions appear as stacked blocks, each comprising a horizontal row with artwork image and descriptive text.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Global alignment is center for the header logo, nav, and page title. Within content, exhibition details are left-aligned, providing a strong text rag and comfortable reading. Horizontal rules span the content width and visually anchor the sections. Social icons are right-aligned, offset from the main content flow.
responsive_hints: Centered header and content column suggest a fluid container that could scale down on smaller viewports while maintaining central alignment.
Two-column exhibition blocks (image + text) are likely to collapse into a single-column stack on mobile (image above text) for readability.
Fixed-position social media buttons on the right edge may shift or dock to bottom on narrow screens to avoid overlap with content.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: The page follows a linear top-to-bottom flow. First, a full-width but visually centered logo block, then a line of navigation links aligned horizontally. After a separating rule, a single centered heading introduces the page ('2025 Exhibitions'). Another rule marks the transition into the main content area. Within this area, exhibitions appear as horizontally oriented blocks: each block has a rectangular artwork image on the left and a right-hand vertical stack of text (title, institution name, date range, and descriptive copy). These exhibition blocks are stacked vertically with consistent spacing between them.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: The upper 20–25% of the viewport is dedicated to branding and navigation, with generous top and bottom padding around the logo and nav. The page title and surrounding whitespace occupy another 10–15% of initial viewport height, creating a pronounced break between global chrome and content. The main content column occupies the central 60–70% of the viewport width, with large left and right margins acting as white framing space. Within exhibition rows, the image appears to occupy roughly 35–40% of the row width, and the text column about 60–65%. Vertical spacing between sections and between exhibition items is noticeably large, promoting a calm, gallery-like feel.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Spacious, editorial layout with ample breathing room between sections and around individual components. Text lines are short to moderate in width, and there are only a few key lines of copy visible per block, leading to low visual density and an emphasis on clarity over information volume per screen.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: The eye moves top-down in a strong vertical flow: first the bold logo, then the horizontal line of navigation links. The horizontal rule under the nav acts as a visual stop, guiding attention down to the centered '2025 Exhibitions' heading. From there, the eye drops further to the first exhibition row, starting with the colorful artwork image on the left (which initially catches attention) and then moving right to read the bold title, institution name, and dates. Social icons on the right form a secondary vertical attractor, but their smaller size and peripheral placement keep them subordinate to the main content.

ui_patterns.visual_description: In the visible area, the only button-like elements are the social media controls: circular outlines with a 1–2px border in a brand color (blue for Facebook, pink for Instagram), white fill, and centered icon glyphs. The circles are perfectly round (50% border-radius) and spaced vertically with consistent gap, likely 16–24px. No filled CTAs or rectangular buttons are shown in this screenshot, implying that the design leans toward minimal, flat, typographic controls rather than heavy button styles.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Social buttons are likely implemented as anchor elements (<a>) within a vertical stack (a flex or simple block container) fixed to the right edge. Each anchor contains an SVG or icon font glyph. Standard CTAs elsewhere on the site (not visible here) would likely be simple <a> or <button> elements styled minimally with border-radius and either outline or flat fill, consistent with the clean aesthetic.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Exhibition entries behave like cards but with a very minimal treatment: no visible border or obvious shadow, just separation through whitespace and alignment. Each card is a horizontal rectangle with an image on the left (sharp corners, no rounded edges, likely full-bleed within its frame) and a text block on the right. The text block has clear vertical spacing: bold title at the top, then lighter subheading, then date line, followed by paragraph copy. Internal padding within the card is generous (approximately 24–32px between content and neighboring elements).

ui_patterns.visual_description: No form fields are visible in the provided screenshot. Given the aesthetic, form controls on this site are likely to be clean, flat inputs with 1px borders in a neutral grey or black, square or slightly rounded corners (2–4px radius), and labels placed above inputs. Error or success states are not evident in this snippet.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Expected structure would be vertically stacked labels and full-width inputs within a form, using simple <label> + <input>/<textarea> pairs and possibly grouped by <fieldset> for more complex forms.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Primary navigation is a horizontal text-only menu placed under the logo and centered. Items are lowercase, likely medium weight, with modest letter spacing and no visible background or border. Menu items that have submenus (e.g., 'what's on', 'about', 'our community') show a small downward chevron or similar indicator next to the text, suggesting a dropdown. The active or hovered state is not directly visible, but given the minimal style, states are likely indicated via underline, text color shift to solid black, or subtle weight change.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Navigation is likely implemented as a semantic <nav> containing an unordered list <ul> with list items <li> and anchors <a>. Dropdowns (where present) would be nested <ul> elements or absolutely positioned submenus, possibly revealed on hover or click. Centering is probably achieved via flexbox or text-align:center on the container.

ui_patterns.visual_description: The exhibition list functions as a vertical stack of media-like items. Each list item (exhibition) has low density, generous vertical spacing, and a clear left-right structure: thumbnail/preview image on the left, text content on the right. There are no visible separators like lines or boxes between items; separation relies solely on whitespace and the consistent structure of each block.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: This is effectively a list of 'media objects' implemented either as a <section> with multiple <article> children or a semantic <ul> where each <li> contains a flexbox row (image + content). CSS flexbox or grid is used to align items horizontally, with gap or margin-bottom defining vertical spacing.

heading.visual_style: Headings appear to use a modern, geometric-to-humanist sans serif with clean lines and slightly rounded shapes. The logo text is bold and striking, while the '2025 Exhibitions' heading is moderately bold with a tall x-height and smooth curves, increasing legibility and giving a contemporary art-gallery feel.

body.visual_style: Body text and navigation appear to use a clean, neutral sans serif with good readability, moderate x-height, and unobtrusive character. The look feels humanist and approachable rather than rigidly geometric.

spacing_system.component_padding: Typical internal padding for content blocks and cards appears to be in the 16–24px range (2–3 base units) on all sides, with slightly larger top/bottom padding for major sections.
spacing_system.section_margin: Vertical spacing between major sections (e.g., between nav and heading, heading and list) appears to be approximately 32–48px (4–6 base units), reinforcing the spacious, gallery-like atmosphere.

interaction_hints: Navigation links likely change color (e.g., from a dark grey to solid black) or gain an underline on hover, with a subtle transition to maintain the minimal aesthetic.

Social media circular buttons likely use a hover effect such as background fill with their brand color, inversion of icon color, or a slight scale-up, accompanied by a short ease-in-out transition (150–200ms).
Exhibition cards may use a very subtle hover state—slight image brightness increase or small upward translation of the entire card—to indicate clickability while preserving the clean layout.
Section transitions and dropdowns, where present, are likely simple fades or slide-downs, with low-friction, fast animations (150–250ms) to keep the interface feeling responsive but unobtrusive.
